pages.full_text:
[postcard photo of Brekatedralen - Nigardsbreen]

Tues 12th August
   We packed up & drove to the centre to see the Akershus Festning – built by Hikon [?Magnusson] c/1300 & Christian IV early [1]7[th], lovely tapestries, wood carved chests, portraits.
   Resistance museum, 3 men ~~[strikethrough]~~ executed outside, one called Rasmussen.
pages.notes: Page has a large glued-in postcard showing Brekatedralen - Nigardsbreen (a Norwegian glacier with a distinctive tent-like visitor centre). Marginal note 'see p.68' written beside the photo on the right side. The author's surname for the builder of Akershus is partially legible — could read 'Magnusson' or similar. Text at right gutter edge is cut off and continues onto the right page. The 'executed' word appears to have a struck-through word before it (possibly 'men' repeated or 'shot'). Year not written on page; this is a Norway trip — volume position suggests late 1990s but year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
We rushed up to Svein's house. He gave us a drink in the garden. Lovely wood panelling & applied arts. His sister is a ceramicist.
   He suggested we go to the beach for the boys to swim from an island to the west of Oslo. [?Hovedøya]? There was a raft & diving board lots of families but not crowded. V. hot & sunny. A marvelous resort so near a capital city. He walked round the island with Simon, & then with me, pointing out the views & islands & rich peoples houses. His wife is very ill. It was very good to spend time with him.
   We came back to the van & had a cup of tea with Svein before he went home.
   We drove on for 1 ¼ hours to [?Fiskum]**
near Kongsberg.
The campsite is a pretty old farm, with old barns & surrounded by orchards. A rifle range & trains.
   Wed 13 Aug
   We came into __Kongsberg__. There is an interesting museum – silver mining/mint/skiing/minerals? Simon bought a stone with a hole
pages.notes: Page number 94 visible in top-right corner. Text continues from left page mid-narrative. The island name to the west of Oslo is partially struck through and uncertain — could be 'Hovedøya' or another name; the original word appears crossed out before the '?' query. Campsite name reads like 'Fiskum' followed by '**' (possibly a footnote marker). The entry for Wed 13 Aug ends mid-sentence ('a stone with a hole') — text continues on the next page. Year not stated on page; Norway context and diary position suggest late 1990s but cannot be confirmed precisely.
